title: [CI:BUILD] [v4.1.1-rhel] Cleanup CI tasks
body:
- Use packit copr tasks for rpm build
- Remove windows installer build task
    
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]



<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

@cevich @edsantiago PTAL

> LGTM with one note: This PR does not address the python-related failures that would normally appear if it weren't for `[CI:BUILD]`. For those, please see #19359

sure, it doesn't address the actual python problem. but it did show up in the `test build rpm` task too IIRC, so it does address that part.

> so it does address that part.

Good deal, I just wanted to mention the other issue in case some future maintainer goes PR-spelunking and is confused by the merge history (unless #19359 merges first).

> > so it does address that part.
> 
> Good deal, I just wanted to mention the other issue in case some future maintainer goes PR-spelunking and is confused by the merge history (unless #19359 merges first).

ack, it's marked as draft so we should be fine. I still need to handle el9 error here which isn't related to the el9 error on main branch.
Since, these are for rhel, would be a good opportunity to actually enable rhel builds on copr instead of centos stream.
Friendly ping.  Is this PR still needed?

> Friendly ping. Is this PR still needed?

let's keep this open. I do want to get rhel packit builds in for maint branches.  I can change the title of the PR for clarity.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Closing for now. unlikely to be worth the effort to bring the rpm to a buildable state.
